diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of chronic kidney disease, bilateral optic nerve colobomas, and a confirmed PAX-2 gene mutation (p.R104X). Prenatal scans indicated bilateral renal hypoplasia, and postnatal clinical and laboratory findings supported chronic kidney disease. The patient also exhibited bilateral vesicoureteral reflux, non-nephrotic proteinuria, and bilateral colobomas with left macular atrophy. Genetic testing confirmed a de novo nonsense mutation in the PAX-2 gene, which is associated with renal-coloboma syndrome.
